title: Smoky Tapioca Stir-fry
description: A fluffy, smoky Filipino dish that is perfect for a sick-day meal, featuring saffron as the main ingredient to add depth and color. This paleo-friendly stir-fry can be enjoyed at any meal time.
instructions: Rinse and soak the tapioca pearls in cold water for at least 30 minutes to remove any grit. Drain well.
Heat a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add oil and swirl to coat.
Add garlic, ginger, and saffron threads to the hot pan. Stir-fry until fragrant (about 1 minute).
Gently add the tapioca pearls to the pan. Cook for about 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
If using fish sauce, add it now. Add soy sauce and continue cooking for another 2 minutes or so until all ingredients are well combined and heated through.
Remove from heat and serve hot.
